title: How do I forget a wireless network on a Windows device?
plain_text: Fix Wi-Fi connection issues on Windows by forgetting and reconnecting to networks. Learn how to remove saved wireless networks from your device. If you are having trouble connecting to a wireless network your device has previously connected to, forgetting the network and reconnecting to it may resolve the issue. Forget the Wi-Fi network on Windows. Note: ITS-managed computers cannot forget NUwave because it is bound to the domain. 1. Select the Start menu. 2. Select Settings > Network & internet. 3. Select Wi-Fi. 4. Select 'Manage known networks'. 5. Select the network you wish to remove. 6. Select Forget. 7. The Wi-Fi network should now be removed from your device.
body_html:
Fix Wi-Fi connection issues on Windows by forgetting and reconnecting to networks. Learn how to remove saved wireless networks from your device. If you are having trouble connecting to a wireless network your device has previously connected to, forgetting the network and reconnecting to it may resolve the issue.

Forget the Wi-Fi network on Windows

Note: ITS-managed computers cannot forget NUwave because it is bound to the domain.

  • Select the Start menu.
  • Select Settings > Network & internet.
  • Select Wi-Fi.
  • Select 'Manage known networks'.
  • Select the network you wish to remove.
  • Select Forget.
  • The Wi-Fi network should now be removed from your device.

The Wi-Fi network should now be removed from your device.
